command: Real-time live updates for the entire frontend dashboard. Currently the dashboard at src/app/tenant/[id]/page.tsx has Supabase Realtime subscriptions for conversations and messages tables, and we just added one for the tenants table (whatsapp_connected). But the following are NOT real-time and only update on manual refresh or 60s polling: (1) contact_rules table - no realtime subscription, (2) knowledge_base table in CapabilitiesTab - no realtime, (3) leads table in LeadsTab - no realtime, (4) calendar/meetings tables in CalendarTab - no realtime, (5) the 60s polling interval only refreshes conversations+messages, NOT tenant data. Goal: make every piece of data in the dashboard update live via Supabase Realtime postgres_changes subscriptions, so the UI is always in sync without manual refresh. Read the relevant files: src/app/tenant/[id]/page.tsx, src/components/tenant/CapabilitiesTab.tsx, src/components/tenant/LeadsTab.tsx, src/components/tenant/CalendarTab.tsx and understand their data fetching patterns. Then implement Realtime subscriptions for all missing tables. The tenantId is always available. All tables have tenant_id column for filtering. Make the changes minimal and surgical - add realtime subscriptions following the existing pattern in page.tsx.

plan.summary: Add Supabase Realtime subscriptions for contact_rules, knowledge_base, leads, calendar, and meetings tables to enable live updates across the entire dashboard.

tasks.instruction: Read src/app/tenant/[id]/page.tsx, src/components/tenant/CapabilitiesTab.tsx, src/components/tenant/LeadsTab.tsx, and src/components/tenant/CalendarTab.tsx to understand current data fetching patterns. Then, add Supabase Realtime subscriptions for contact_rules, knowledge_base, leads, calendar, and meetings tables in their respective components, following the existing pattern in page.tsx (use tenantId for filtering, handle subscription lifecycle with useEffect). Ensure subscriptions are minimal and surgical, replacing any polling or manual refresh logic.
tasks.context: TenantId is always available. All tables have tenant_id column. Existing subscriptions in page.tsx for conversations and messages serve as reference. Goal is live updates without 60s polling or manual refresh.

tasks.instruction: Verify that the Supabase database has Realtime enabled for contact_rules, knowledge_base, leads, calendar, and meetings tables. If not, provide SQL commands to enable it (e.g., ALTER TABLE ... REPLICA IDENTITY FULL). Also, ensure RLS policies allow authenticated users to subscribe to these tables based on tenant_id.
tasks.context: Multi-tenant isolation is critical. Tables must have Realtime enabled and proper RLS for real-time subscriptions to work. Reference existing tables like conversations and messages for patterns.

tasks.instruction: Review the real-time subscription implementations for contact_rules, knowledge_base, leads, calendar, and meetings tables to ensure multi-tenant data isolation. Verify that subscriptions filter strictly by tenant_id and that no data leaks are possible. Also, check RLS policies on these tables to confirm they restrict access to the correct tenant.
tasks.context: TenantId must be used in all subscription filters. Ensure no cross-tenant data exposure via Realtime channels.

tasks.instruction: Design test cases for real-time updates across all new subscriptions: contact_rules, knowledge_base, leads, calendar, and meetings. Include scenarios for data insertion, update, deletion, and multi-tenant isolation. Also, verify that existing subscriptions (conversations, messages, tenants) still work and that polling intervals are removed or disabled.
tasks.context: Test in a multi-tenant environment to ensure updates are live and isolated. Check for performance regressions or memory leaks from multiple subscriptions.

tasks.instruction: After changes are implemented, run npm run build to verify no errors, then deploy to Vercel. Also, check Supabase Realtime configuration to ensure new tables are properly enabled for subscriptions.
tasks.context: Deploy after every task as per user preference. Ensure build passes before deployment.
